perturbation.original_text: Instead of fixed compensation, the Affiliate receives result- dependent Advertising Cost Compensation (also known in the element 5 Control Panel as "Commission") in exchange for publishing the advertisements. The Advertising Cost Compensation depends on the actual sales generated by end users referred via the electronic advertisement (the Affiliate's link).
perturbation.changed_text: Instead of fixed compensation, the Affiliate receives consideration in exchange for publishing the advertisements. The amount of consideration, if any, will be determined at element 5's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a significant contradiction. The original text clearly states that the Affiliate receives 'Advertising Cost Compensation', which is directly tied to sales. The modified text replaces this with 'consideration' determined at element 5's 'sole discretion', negating the result-dependent nature of the compensation. This makes the original references to Advertising Cost Compensation in Section II. § 6, Advertising Cost Compensation (1), (3), (4), (5), meaningless and unenforceable, as there is no longer a defined or agreed-upon method for calculation or payment.
perturbation.location: I. Object of the Affiliate Program/ Affiliate Management Program for Software Publishers
perturbation.contradicted_location: II. Affiliate Program § 6 Advertising Cost Compensation (1)
perturbation.contradicted_text: element 5 will pay Affiliate its advertising costs (including Vat, if any, on presentation of a valid VAT invoice) if an end user arrives via the link integrated in Affiliate's Web site at the order page hosted by element 5 for the respective Software Source: SOUTHERN STAR ENERGY INC., SB-2/A, 12/2/2005 Publisher's Product under this Agreement and end user uses the automatic ordering system that carries out the registration and payment processing, so that the Affiliate's link is deemed to be directly causative for the entry of the End User into the agreement with the Software Publisher and End User's payment, proving that all requirements are irrevocably met ("agreements arising in a qualified manner"). Affiliate is only eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement and fees earned up to the date termination will remain payable only if the related orders are not cancelled or returned.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a clear compensation structure ('Advertising Cost Compensation' based on sales) with 'consideration' at element 5's 'sole discretion'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states that 'element 5 will pay Affiliate its advertising costs' based on a defined process involving end-user actions via the Affiliate's link. The change removes the previously defined method for calculating compensation, rendering the original payment terms unenforceable and creating a significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: element 5 will pay the Affiliate the Advertising Cost Compensation as defined in II. § 6 . Further invoicing terms are obtained from the existing business relationship, defined in the respective share-it! Developer Agreement between Software Publisher and element 5.
perturbation.changed_text: element 5 will handle invoicing according to internal procedures. Details will not be shared with the Affiliate.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction because the original text explicitly states that the Advertising Cost Compensation is defined in Section II.§ 6, implying a clear method and the invoicing terms are tied to a share-it agreement, creating transparency. The modified text removes the reference to Advertising Cost Compensation and II. § 6 and replaces this with a vague reference to 'internal procedures' not shared with the affiliate, creating uncertainty about whether there will be any compensation and about the calculation or verification of invoices. The fact that it won't be shared with the affiliate contradicts with the purpose of this agreement and introduces a contradiction to the full context of the agreement.
perturbation.location: III. Affiliate Management for Software Publishers § 3 element 5's Service Fee
perturbation.contradicted_location: II. Affiliate Program § 6 Advertising Cost Compensation (6)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) The payment of the Advertising Cost Compensation shall occur monthly insofar as the sum due exceeds a value of 100 EUR / 100 USD. If this is not the case, the sum due can be retained until a total value of at least 100 EUR / 100 USD is reached, at the latest, upon termination of the Affiliate's participation in the Program.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that compensation is defined in Section II.§ 6 and tied to a share-it agreement, while the changed text states that element 5 will handle invoicing according to internal procedures, which will not be shared with the Affiliate. This contradicts the contradicted text (6), which outlines the process for payment of Advertising Cost Compensation. The change removes transparency and introduces uncertainty about whether compensation will be paid, directly conflicting with the payment terms outlined in the contradicted section. The definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' fits this case as essential language is removed in one section (reference to Section II.§ 6), creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections (payment of advertising costs).

perturbation.original_text: element 5 RESERVES THE RIGHT TO MODIFY AND TO SUPPLEMENT THESE GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS AT ANY TIME. THE CURRENT VERSION WILL BE MAINTAINED FOR VIEWING AS A HYPERLINK ON element 5's WEB SITE IN THE CONTROL PANEL. MOREOVER, element 5 WILL ANNOUNNOUNCE ANY CHANGES TO THESE TERMS VIA E- MAIL. WHITHIN 14 DAYS OF THE ANNOUNCEMENT OF CHANGED PARTICIPATION TERMS, AFFILIATE AND SOFTWARE PUBLISHER MAY OBJECT TO SUCH CHANGES. IF NO OBJECTION IS RECEIVED DURING THIS TIME, THE PARTICIPANT IS DEEMED TO HAVE ACCEPTED THE CHANGES AND THEY WILL BECOME PART OF THE EXISTING RELATIONS UNDER THE AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: element 5 reserves the right to modify these terms at any time. Notice of changes will be at element 5's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text outlines a clear process for modifying the terms, including maintaining a current version online, announcing changes via email, and allowing a 14-day objection period. The modified text removes these specific obligations, stating that notice of changes is at element 5's 'discretion'. This contradicts earlier sections (like II § 6) that rely on the General Terms and Conditions being stable and predictable. By removing the defined process for notification and consent, it becomes unclear how changes to key aspects like 'Advertising Cost Compensation' or 'termination' will be communicated or agreed upon, creating a significant contradiction. This makes enforcement of these sections questionable since modifications might be made without proper notification.
perturbation.location: IV. General terms § 2 Changes to these provisions
perturbation.contradicted_location: IV. General terms § 7 Agreement period / Cancellation
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement will begin upon acceptance of Affiliate's Program application and will end when terminated by either party of this Agreement. This Agreement is entered into for an unlimited period of time. Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific process for modifying the terms, including email notification and a 14-day objection period. The changed text removes these specific requirements, stating that notice of changes is at element 5's 'discretion'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that either party may cancel the agreement 'through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel'. The removal of mandatory email notification for changes, and the introduction of 'discretion', creates uncertainty about whether changes to termination clauses will be communicated effectively, thus contradicting the initial clear communication methods, making it difficult to enforce.
